title: Clean Fuels Submits Comments to CARB Following Land Use Change Public Forum
content: Clean Fuels Alliance America, together with California Advanced Biofuels Alliance (CABA), submitted formal comments to CARB after the agencyâ€™s public forum on biofuels and land use change. In their comments, they stressed the need to base land-use change modeling on the most up-to-date science â€” reflecting improvements in agricultural productivity, more accurate economic and carbon models â€” rather than outdated assumptions. â€œOur members represent the majority of the clean diesel fuel used in California today, and we remain committed to working collaboratively with CARB to ensure the LCFS continues to deliver real, near-term emissions reductions,â€ said Cory-Ann Wind, Director of State Regulatory Affairs for Clean Fuels. â€œModernizing land use change modeling is a critical step toward aligning policy with todayâ€™s data and market realities.â€ Clean Fuels and CABA urged CARB to initiate a land use change model modernization effort in 2026, maintain appropriate treatment of waste-based feedstocks and provide clarity for emerging oilseed crops that can deliver additional climate benefits without expanding cropland.
author: Analysts Account
